key_insight: FEMONet learns complex optical scattering by predicting Galerkin-consistent finite-element coefficients in a parameterized operator space, rather than unconstrained field values.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, more generalizable surrogate simulation for nanophotonic analysis and inverse design across diverse dielectric, metallic, plasmonic, arrayed, and 3D structures.
method: Encodes the scattering problem in an operator parameter space of physical entities.
Uses the variational weak form to connect parameters to the solution space.
Predicts finite-element expansion coefficients, with derivatives handled by assembled stiffness matrices and load vectors.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports high accuracy, stable training, and broad generalization across multiple complex nanophotonic structure classes, including 3D cases.
watch_item: Performance likely depends on the quality of the finite-element formulation and mesh/discretization choices, which can affect training stability and portability.
paper_title: Learning light scattering from operator parameter spaces to Galerkin-consistent solution spaces
